summary: Iteration 10 regression (6-feature batch on top of iter9). NEW test_iter10_regression.py: 15/15 PASS after one inline backend fix. Coverage: (1) Items name+machine_number round-trip on Car/Motorcycle/Pezadu — Pezadu FAILED initially because PezaduIn model was missing the two fields; FIXED in server.py (added name + machine_number with empty defaults). (2) Contracts: days_overdue (int) and penalty_paid present; days_overdue=5 -> status='overdue'; days_overdue=15 -> status='auction_ready'. (3) Payments: overdue_penalty_only clears penalty (penalty_paid==full penalty, remaining=0); overdue_interest_pen zeros both penalty + interest_remaining; overdue_full closes contract (status='redeemed') and zeros everything. (4) Capital Sources (POST /api/funding-sources): rate=5 + term_months=12 persists. (5) Auctions mark_sold with explicit interest_fee=200 -> auction.interest_fee=200, cash_portion=800; finance/summary deltas: auction_sales +1000, auction_interest_profit +200, auction_tax_collected +100, net_profit +~200. Invoice generated with subtotal=1000 tax=100 total=1100. Buyer-facing invoice PDF asserted to NOT contain 'Interest Fee' (both byte and pypdf text extraction). (6) Auction mark_sold WITHOUT interest_fee -> auto-computed from contract interest_remaining + penalty (capped at sold_price). Regression: iter9 full suite re-ran 49/49 PASS. Frontend Playwright spot-check (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL): admin login -> dashboard; /items 'New' dialog on Car, Motorcycle, AND Pezadu tabs each shows item-{kind}-name + item-{kind}-machine_number; /contracts Pre-Auction amber card visible with '41 Pre-Auction' / '3 Auction Ready' badges + days-overdue chips + 'auction ready' status badge; /payments shows tab-all-payments + tab-overdue-payments + overdue-payment-btn; opening the Overdue Payment dialog shows all 3 mode buttons (mode-full, mode-interest-penalty, mode-penalty) plus overdue-amount auto-fill; /finance shows finance-tab-calculator + capital-rate/capital-term/capital-interest-preview inside capital New dialog + calc-principal/calc-rate/calc-months/calc-period/calc-total-interest/calc-total-repay/calc-monthly on calculator tab; client-payment-summary testid is correctly conditional on viewPayments.length>0 (verified in code).

minor.issue: FIXED THIS ITERATION: PezaduIn pydantic model was missing 'name' and 'machine_number' fields. Pydantic v2 default 'ignore' mode silently dropped them, so the frontend (Items.js pezaduFields) was sending name+machine_number for every new heavy-equipment item but the values never made it to MongoDB. Server.py patched (lines 362-371) — both fields added with empty string defaults. Verified with round-trip test_pezadu_name_and_machine_number_roundtrip.

issues: Recharts ResponsiveContainer still emits width(-1)/height(-1) warnings on first paint — carryover from iter6+, cosmetic only.

design_issues.screen: Several dialogs (Items New, Capital New, Overdue Payment)
issues: Radix DialogContent emits 'Missing Description or aria-describedby' a11y warning. Add <DialogDescription> (sr-only allowed) inside each DialogContent or pass aria-describedby={undefined} explicitly to silence.

action_items: RESOLVED inline: PezaduIn now has name + machine_number fields. Please leave this as-is.

OPTIONAL: emptyFor('pezadu') in /app/frontend/src/pages/Items.js (lines 121-138) and emptyFor() vehicle branch (140+) DO NOT initialize 'name' or 'machine_number' to ''. Works because typing into a controlled Input sets it, but for symmetry with the field list add name:'' and machine_number:'' to both empty objects.
OPTIONAL: Add <DialogDescription className='sr-only'>...</DialogDescription> to all DialogContent that don't already have one to fix the Radix a11y warning seen in console.
OPTIONAL: Wrap Recharts ResponsiveContainer in a div with min-h-[300px] (or fixed height) to silence width(-1)/height(-1) warnings on first paint — affects Dashboard + Finance.
OPTIONAL: Note that overdue_full payment sets contract status to 'redeemed' (not 'closed'/'paid'/'completed'). Make sure UI status badges / filters know about this canonical name.

OPTIONAL: Capital Sources endpoints live at /api/funding-sources (NOT /api/finance/capital-sources). UI in Finance.js correctly calls /finance-sources via apiClient — just flagging for any external integrators.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: PezaduIn missing name + machine_number — silent data loss on save (Pydantic v2 'ignore' default). Lesson: when adding new optional fields to multiple Item models, add them to ALL kinds simultaneously, AND prefer model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid') on input models so unknown fields raise 422 instead of being silently dropped (current 'ignore' default hides typos and missing schema updates).

server.py is now ~2500 lines and growing each iteration (Items+Contracts+Payments+Auctions+Finance+Reports+Settings+WhatsApp+Backups+Public). Strongly recommend splitting into APIRouter modules before iter11. Repeated pattern: every new feature touches 1-2 Pydantic models, 1-2 endpoints, plus _recompute_contract_status — currently scattered.

_recompute_contract_status (server.py 582-685) is the canonical place for derived contract state — penalty_paid, days_overdue, status (active/overdue/auction_ready/redeemed). It is invoked from EVERY list/get/payment/auction path. Good. But it MUTATES the dict in place and writes back to db — make sure the cron/scheduled job (if any) doesn't race with API writes on the same contract.

Invoice PDF (pdf_utils.build_invoice_pdf, lines 508-600) correctly shows only subtotal+tax+total. _internal_interest_fee / _internal_cash_portion are stored on the invoice doc for finance summary use but never rendered to buyers. Confirmed by both byte search and pypdf text extraction in test.

AuctionSoldIn.interest_fee Optional[float]; mark_sold caps interest_fee at sold_price (good — prevents negative cash_portion) and falls back to (interest_remaining + penalty) when omitted. Auto-compute behaviour is sound.
FundingSourceIn.term_months Optional[int] persists; consider validating range 6-12 to match the UI dropdown (currently backend accepts any int incl. negative or huge values).
Overdue payment 'overdue_full' sets contract.status='redeemed'. Document this status — it's not in the typical {active, overdue, auction_ready, auction, sold} mental model from earlier iterations.

On clients page modal: client-payment-summary card is correctly gated by viewPayments.length>0. Consider showing an empty-state ('No payments yet') instead of hiding entirely so the user knows the section exists.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py — PezaduIn now includes name + machine_number fields (was silently dropping them from frontend payload)
/app/backend/tests/test_iter10_regression.py — NEW (15 tests across 7 classes, covers all 6 new features + regression)

success_rate.frontend: 100% — login, items name+machine_number on all 3 vehicle tabs, contracts Pre-Auction card, payments tabs+overdue dialog 3-modes, finance capital dropdowns+preview, loan calculator tab full grid

seed_data_creation: Per-run TEST_iter10 client (BI), several TEST_iter10 cars (auto-deleted by fixture), a TEST_iter10 motorcycle, TEST_iter10 Komatsu forklift FD25T pezadu (auto-deleted). For overdue tests, several short-lived contracts at days_ago_due=5 and =15 — all torn down via fixture. Also one TEST_iter10 capital source (funding-source) with rate=5 term_months=12 — deleted. Two auctions sold with buyer 'TEST_iter10 Buyer' and 'TEST_iter10 Buyer Auto' — invoices remain in db (no delete endpoint exercised). No persistent pollution after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter10 covers 6 new features atop iter9. Key gotchas: (1) PezaduIn was missing name + machine_number — FIXED in this iter. Any new Item kind in future should be checked against the frontend field list. (2) Overdue payment types are 'overdue_full', 'overdue_interest_pen', 'overdue_penalty_only' (NOT 'overdue_interest_only'). (3) 'overdue_full' transitions contract.status to 'redeemed' (not 'closed'/'paid'). (4) Capital sources endpoint is /api/funding-sources NOT /finance/capital-sources. (5) Auction creation = POST /api/auctions/move (NOT /api/auctions); payload uses 'starting_price' (NOT 'listing_price'). (6) auction.interest_fee defaults to contract (interest_remaining+penalty) when payload omits it; capped at sold_price. (7) Invoice PDF must never show 'Interest Fee' — buyer-facing. _internal_interest_fee/_internal_cash_portion live on the invoice doc but not in the PDF. (8) /finance/summary new keys: auction_interest_profit, auction_tax_collected. (9) Contract list/get now returns days_overdue (int) and penalty_paid (float) — computed in _recompute_contract_status. (10) Status >10 days overdue = 'auction_ready', 1-10 days = 'overdue'. (11) Frontend client-payment-summary testid is conditional on viewPayments.length>0 — test against a client with payments (e.g., the TEST_iter10 client used in overdue tests has 3 payments after iter10 run).
rca of the issue: PezaduIn was missing 'name' and 'machine_number' attributes. Frontend Items.js pezaduFields() (lines 77-103) sends both fields in the payload, but the backend POST /api/items/{kind} runs model(**payload).model_dump() on PezaduIn. Pydantic v2's default behaviour for unknown fields is 'ignore' (silently drop) — no 422 was raised, the doc just got saved without name/machine_number, and the user thought it worked. RCA: when adding new optional fields across multiple Item Pydantic models in one iteration, PezaduIn was missed (only CarIn and MotorcycleIn got updated). Fix: added 'name: str = ""' and 'machine_number: str = ""' to PezaduIn in server.py (lines 362-371). Long-term: switch all *In models to model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid') so missing schema updates raise 422 instead of silent data loss; this is especially important when the same wire-format dict is reused across multiple resource types.
